title: gypsophila perfoliata f. latifolia
intro: Gypsophila perfoliata f. latifolia, also known as Buck's beard or perfoliate gypsophila, is a flowering plant belonging to the carnation family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Gypsophila perfoliata f. latifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive issues, and alleviate anxiety and stress, making it a valuable herbal remedy. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are typically used, often in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures.
